text: The surgeon begins the laparoscopic adhesiolysis using a disposable shearer, which simultaneously coagulates and cuts tissue. The shearer's blades are designed to minimize lateral thermal spread during the procedure.
The surgeon gently pushes the omentum downwards, taking care to avoid the underlying intestines, while maintaining a bloodless plane through precise dissection.
The surgeon carefully separates the uterus from adhesions on the anterior abdominal wall, using a Foley's catheter inserted in the bladder to prevent injury during the dissection.
The surgeon completes the dissection of bladder adhesions from the uterus, restoring its mobility. The next step involves dissecting adhesions at the level of the adnexa.
The surgeon carefully dissects dense omental adhesions at the level of the adnexa, taking precautions to avoid excessive coagulation that could potentially harm the ovarian reserve.
The surgeon completes the procedure by liberating all adhesions, ensuring the anatomical structures are free from restrictive tissue connections.
